name: pr_tms_ls_fwd
drl:
rule RJ when T0(f0 == true) then insertLogical(new T1("v", true)); end
rule ROBS when T1($x : f0, $y : f1) then end
_finding: Logical THEN stated of an equal fact (external review round 15, the API-found interleaving): under the pinned identity assertBehaviour a stated insert always MINTS a new handle even onto a live justified equality-key - two T1 facts coexist (the ird_l2 recon rung, now corpus-graduated in the external-ops shape). Deleting the justification then retracts ONLY the justified one; the stated twin survives. Downstream rules genuinely see two facts in this order - order-dependence is the certified semantics, not a defect.
